mnemonics.title: Parse Tree vs AST
mnemonics.content: PArse Tree = ALL details (Concrete), AST = Abstract (Essential)

mnemonics.title: TAC Operands
mnemonics.content: TAC = Three Address Code: Max 2 Sources + 1 Destination

mnemonics.title: Attribute Flow
mnemonics.content: Synthesized = Rise UP (bottom-up), Inherited = Come DOWN (top-down)

mnemonics.title: Quadruple Fields
mnemonics.content: Quad = 4 fields: OAR - Operator, Arg1, Arg2, Result

mnemonics.title: DAG Benefit
mnemonics.content: DAG = Detect And Eliminate Common subexpressions by sharing nodes

acronyms.usage: Tree representation of program structure without syntactic details
Intermediate code with maximum 3 operands per instruction
IR with shared nodes for common subexpressions
Framework for attaching semantic rules to grammar productions
Machine-independent code between source and target
Attribute computed from child node values (bottom-up flow)
Attribute computed from parent/sibling values (top-down flow)

visualTips.visualization: Draw an inverted tree: root at top (start symbol), branches down to terminals. Every internal node is a non-terminal, every leaf is a terminal.
Show nested boxes representing program structure: if-statement contains condition, then-part, else-part as child nodes.
Draw a tree where identical subtrees merge into single node with multiple parent pointers.
Imagine operands pushed to stack; when operator encountered, pop two, compute, push result back.
Process expression tree left-to-right: left subtree gets temporary t1, right subtree gets t2, parent combines in t3.

analogies.analogy: Parse tree is like a detailed recipe with every step including 'preheat oven to 350°F'. AST is like a summary: 'prepare cake batter' - essential steps only.
DAG is like a shared library - if two programs need the same function, they reference the same library copy instead of each having their own.
Intermediate code is like a universal translator between two people speaking different languages - converts ideas to a common format before final translation.
TAC is like breaking a complex math expression into separate calculations on paper, each step showing one operation clearly.
Inherited attributes are like family traits passed from parents to children - information flows downward in the family tree.
